title: Condaliopsis lloydii
taxonomy: Condaliopsis lloydii is a genus of flowering plants in the family Boraginaceae. It is a member of the tribe Cynoglosseae and is native to Mexico. The classification of Condaliopsis lloydii places it within the kingdom Plantae, and further into the division Magnoliophyta. The plant's classification also includes the class Magnoliopsida and the order Borales. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants.

intro: Condaliopsis lloydii, also known as Lloyd's Condalia, is a shrub or small tree species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Rhamnaceae family. Traditionally, Lloyd's Condalia has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lloyd's Condalia is commonly used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and gastrointestinal problems.
